title: Benzoyl Peroxide + Tretinoin
reason: Benzoyl peroxide oxidises tretinoin on contact, rendering it inactive. Never layer them in the same session.
tip: AM: benzoyl peroxide. PM: tretinoin only, after it fully absorbs.

title: Benzoyl Peroxide + Retinol
reason: Benzoyl peroxide degrades retinol through oxidation, completely eliminating its anti-ageing efficacy.
tip: Use in strictly separate AM/PM sessions — never in the same layer.

title: Benzoyl Peroxide + Vitamin C
reason: Benzoyl peroxide can oxidise ascorbic acid, reducing vitamin C's brightening and antioxidant effect.
tip: AM: vitamin C. PM or separate session: benzoyl peroxide.

title: Retinoid + BHA
reason: Both are potent exfoliants. Combining tretinoin with salicylic acid can cause excessive irritation and barrier disruption.
tip: AM: salicylic acid. PM: tretinoin only.

title: Retinoid + AHA (Glycolic)
reason: Glycolic acid increases tretinoin absorption and risk of irritation. Use glycolic strictly in AM and tretinoin strictly at night.
tip: AM: glycolic acid. PM: tretinoin only. Never same session.

title: Retinoid + AHA (Lactic)
reason: Combining lactic acid with tretinoin significantly raises irritation risk, especially for sensitive or retinoid-naive skin.
tip: Alternate nights — never apply both in the same session.

title: Retinol + BHA
reason: Salicylic acid lowers skin pH, destabilising retinol and increasing irritation. Keep them in separate sessions.

title: Retinol + AHA (Glycolic)
reason: Both promote cell turnover — layering them risks over-exfoliation, redness, and prolonged sensitivity.
tip: Alternate nights. Do not layer in the same session.

title: Retinol + AHA (Lactic) — mild
reason: Lactic acid is gentler than glycolic, but combining with retinol still elevates irritation risk for sensitive or retinol-naive skin.
tip: Generally fine for experienced users — avoid if your skin reacts easily.

title: Vitamin C + Niacinamide — low concern
reason: Historically thought to cause flushing (nicotinic acid formation), but modern stable formulations show minimal interaction. Low concern.
tip: Generally safe together. Separate by 30 min if you notice any flushing.
